mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding aggregate income and consumption, which raises corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in months when PAYEMS increases, or the Rank IC between PAYEMS change and 20-day returns is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, leading to downward revisions in corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, or the signal's hit rate falls below 45% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales confirm a demand-supply expansion loop where factory output is absorbed by consumer spending, amplifying earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1.0%, an increase in CPIAUCSL signals nascent inflation that reduces real earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not negative when CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing output, which reduces aggregate supply and corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when INDPRO declines, or the Rank IC between INDPRO changes and 5-day returns is positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms broad-based demand expansion, drives revenue growth across sectors and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC greater than 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1,000 thousand units, the contraction in residential investment spills over to consumer durable spending and construction employment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when HOUST is below 1,000, or the signal's hit rate is below 40% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor income and weakens near-term consumption forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Increasing UNRATE does not produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC below -0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.
